post_rendered:
Welcome to Mafia Game 303 / Newbie Game 112


North Carolina Mafia

good times never seemed so good…


- Important Rules -
This tab summarizes the most important rules for easy reference.

For experienced players: everything you need is here, and in the setup tab.
For new players: you may find it useful to familiarize yourself with the rest of the rules.

Integrity
  • Do not edit or delete your posts, even for typos. Just post again.
  • Do not OOT (Out of Thread) and talk about this game outside this thread, your role PM, or approved private chats, even if you are dead. Access to spectator chats will be granted upon request.
  • Do not gamethrow or intentionally sabotage your wincondition.
  • Do not attach files or link to external documents concerning this game. Discussions must stay in this thread.
  • Do not use OGI (Out of Game Information). Publicly discussed reads should be made exclusively from information in this thread or past completed games.
  • To confirm your understanding of the rules, send your favorite fact you know about North Carolina in your role PM.

Activity
  • Each "Day" is 12 days while each "Night" is 2 days.
  • Do not post during night.
  • This game uses Dawn start: Day 1 will not start until everyone shows that they have read the rules by completing the confirm task.
  • All times will be in Eastern Standard Time (EST, UTC-5) or Eastern Daylight Time (EDT, UTC-4) as applicable.
  • Activity rules are 48 hours to first PM prod, 72 hours to second PM prod, and 96 hours to replacement status.

Voting
  • You must format your votes correctly. Capitalization does not matter, and capital i/lowercase L are not differentiated.
  • Both bold vote and code vote formats are accepted, although bold is preferred. Refer to the Voting tab if you do not know what these are.
  • Majority hang is off for Day 1, but it is on starting Day 2.
  • Deadline hang is on from Day 1 but it is off for LYLO.
  • Votes are locked at LYLO.

Etiquette and Warnings
  • Use common sense.
  • Do not use formatting that makes text hard to read.
  • You may attack a player’s opinion, but do not make personal attacks on players themselves.
  • Disagreements should be resolved in a way that does not affect gameplay.
  • Do not discuss rule violations by other players in the thread; contact the moderators privately.
  • First-timers can receive up to 3 warnings before their next offense results in a force replacement or modk!ll. All other players can receive up to 2.
  • Players may be replaced at the moderators' discretion for procedural difficulties.
  • All other rules are standard.

Setup and Moderators
  • This game is based on the Matrix6 setup.
  • Mafia have access to daychat.
  • All role mechanics and Night Action Resolutions are standard.
  • This game is moderated by ostriches88 and duke_of_wedgewood.
  • This game operates optional discord spectator and mafia private chats using the Unpaid Intern Central server. Contact the mods to request access.

Resources and Support
  • For any issues, you may contact us through your role PM or through Discord (@asiago8, @duke_86659).
  • For a list of basic resources, refer to the Resources tab below.
  • For a more extensive list of resources compiled by the UIMT, go here.
  • If this game is causing you significant stress or frustration, feel free to use your role PM to talk or vent. We may not be allowed to give you technical advice, but we can listen or help you find a better way to process events. You may also contact us via Discord if you prefer.

  • General
    Credits to Binomial-theorem, chaotic_iak, willwang123, and fdas, among others, as well as the Mafia Universe forum rules, for the rule template.

    The following are standard mafia game rules.

    • Do not post during the night phase.
    • Do not talk about this game's events outside of this thread unless you are specifically given permission to do so by the moderators. Details are in the Moderation Policies tab.
    • Do not talk about other ongoing games in this thread. Referring to anything that has happened in an ongoing game, even if you do not say which game it is, is OOT.
    • If you are dead, do not post, and do not talk about this game anywhere else. You may still spectate; access to spoiled and unspoiled spectator chats will be offered.
    • Do not use any formatting that obscures or conceals text. This includes [color=#FFFFFF], [color=white], and [color=transparent].
    • Do not edit or delete posts, even for typos: what goes in the thread stays in the thread. Instead, use EBWOP or EBWON as appropriate.
    • Do not quote or pretend to quote any PMs from the mods, including role PMs.
    • Do not impersonate the mods.
    • Do not lie to the mods.
    • Do not abuse loopholes in the rules. Intent will be considered during rule enforcement.
    • You may use your role PM to ask questions, such as rule clarifications.

  • Votes and Hangs
    This tab covers voting, unvoting, No Hangs, and hangs.

    • Votes must be in this format to be counted: Vote: [player username].
    • By default, votes should be made in [b] (bold) tags. However, votes made in [code] and [c] tags will also be accepted.
    • Usernames in votes must be spelled, but not necessarily capitalized, correctly. Do not use nicknames.
    • For the purposes of voting, I (capital i) and l (lowercase L) are considered identical.
    • For example, both Vote: mathjay and Vote: mAtHjAy are valid votes, but vote: mathjay, Vote: jay, Vote: mathjay., Vote:mathjay, Vote: mathjay, and Vote: mathjay are invalid.
    • Votes followed by extraneous punctuation that is similarly formatted, even with separate tags, are invalid.
    • Votes in hide, tip, or quote tags do not count.
    • Vote to hang no one by posting Vote: No Hang.
    • Unvote by posting Unvote. You do not have to unvote before voting again.
    • Votecounts will be posted periodically. Players may request a new VC in bold or in their role PM.
    • Invalid votes will not count under any circumstances. Consider notifying the mods in your role PM if you want to purposely fakevote.
    • Valid votes will always be counted unless they are clearly unintentional. There is no guarantee that an accidental vote will not be counted.
    • Votes will be carried over if a player is replaced.
    • The Not Voting section should be ignored if it contradicts another part of the votecount (VC).
    • If an error in the votecount is not pointed out within 2 days or by the next EOD, whichever is sooner, the incorrect VC will become official.
    • This game uses deadline hang: the player with the most votes at the EOD (End of Day) deadline will be hanged.
    • If two or more players have the same number of votes at the EOD deadline, the player who reached that number of votes first will be hanged. Details are in the Moderation Policies tab.
    • If no votes have been cast by the deadline, no one will be hanged.
    • Starting Day 2, if a player is voted by the majority of living players, then that player will be hanged immediately; and if No Hang is voted by half of living players, a No Hang will occur.
    • Upon reaching LYLO (Hang-or-Lose), votes will be locked, meaning that they cannot be changed.
    • Deadline hang is off at LYLO. If a majority hang is not reached by the deadline, the day will end without a hang, resulting in an automatic win for the mafia team.
    • The moment the deadline occurs, no further votes will be counted. Details are in the Moderation Policies tab.
    • If the deadline has passed or a hammer has occurred and the EOD has not been declared, the game will proceed into twilight. Players may still post, but no more votes or unvotes will be counted.
    • Once the moderators declare the EOD, the person hanged will be officially eliminated and their role revealed. Night will then immediately begin.
  • Activity
    This tab covers deadlines, activity rules, V/LAs, and replacements.

    • All times will be in Eastern Standard Time (EST, UTC-5) or Eastern Daylight Time (EDT, UTC-4) as applicable, unless otherwise noted.
    • Each day phase will be 288 hours (12 days), and each night phase will be 48 hours (2 days).
    • The moderators may extend deadlines at their discretion.
    • The current deadline can be found in the bottom of the OP (Original Post, or post #1).
    • If a player with an NA (Night Action) anticipates being unavailable during the night phase, they may submit any night actions to be performed in advance.
    • If a player with an NA has not submitted their action by 24 hours before the night deadline, a reminder will be sent.
    • If the moderators do not receive an NA submission from a player by the night deadline, the player will not perform an action that night.
    • Night results will be sent after the night deadline.
    • Night will end when the moderators make a post declaring the end of night (EON). You may not post in the thread until then, even if the night deadline has passed. Instead, PM the mods for updates if they miss a deadline.
    • Players who are temporarily unable to fulfill activity requirements can declare a V/LA (Vacation/Long Absence) by posting the V/LA dates in bold in-thread or in their role PM. Players on a V/LA will not be prodded.
    • V/LAs should be shorter than one week. For longer absences, contact the moderators for options.
    • Players who no longer wish to or are unable to play in this game can request replacement by posting Request Replacement in-thread or in their role PM.
    • Players who have replaced out generally will not be allowed to replace back in.
    • Players will receive their first PM prod if they have not made a game-advancing post for 48 hours and are not on V/LA. Details on "game-advancing post" are in the Moderation Policies tab.
    • Players will receive their second PM prod if they have not made a game-advancing post for 72 hours and are not on V/LA.
    • Players will be replaced if they have not made a game-advancing post for 96 hours and are not on V/LA, unless they post before the moderators find a replacement.
    • If a player receives 4 PM Prods, they will be replaced.
    • If a player reaches replacement status more than once, they will be replaced.
    • Activity Checks (ACs) will be posted periodically. Players may request a new AC in bold or in their role PM.
    • Activity will reset overnight. Players will not receive prods between twilight and daystart.
    • If you have access to a private topic, you may request that a partner be poked. The moderators will send a notification via PM to the specified player, but this will not count as an official prod.
    • If a player is postbanned by the AoPS Sheriff, they will be replaced. Details are in the Moderation Policies tab.
  • Integrity and Etiquette
    This tab covers integrity recommendations, player etiquette, warning limits, and examples of inconsiderate gameplay.

    • Do not gamethrow.
    • Do not discuss player alignments based on V/LAs, replacements, warnings, force replacements, modk!lls, or other interactions with moderators.
    • Do not discuss rule violations, especially violations by other players in the thread. If you wish, contact the moderators privately through your role PM or Discord.
    • Any significant, game-related, and intentionally exclusive player-to-player communication outside of moderator-sanctioned channels is prohibited.
    • Do not publicly reference OGI (Out of Game Information) in reads or create situations outside the thread that players could reasonably consider alignment indicative.

    The above rules are attempts to preserve the spirit of the game and should not affect most players. For specific details and examples, click here.

    • Do not make personal attacks. You may attack a player's opinion, but do not demean the player themselves.
    • Do not spam.
    • Follow AoPS Community Guidelines and the AoPS Terms of Service.
    • Be mature and use common sense.
    • Behavior that disrupts the game for players and makes it unreasonably hard for them to read and play may result in a warning.
      Causing significant disruptions to gameplay may result in a force replacement at the moderators' discretion.
    • For minor rule infractions, first-timers can receive up to three warnings, while all other players can receive up to two. A player may receive multiple warnings for a single infraction.

    The above rules broadly address most etiquette issues. For specific details and examples of improper behavior, click here.

  • - Moderation Policies -
  • Moderator Information
    This tab provides technical information about this game.

    • ostriches88 and duke_of_wedgewood are the moderators of Mafia Game 303.
    • Other interns have contributed to flavor, setup, OCRSTM, feedback, and management of the private chat discord system.
    • This game uses the Unpaid Intern Central consolidated private chat system. Participants of this game will be granted access as appropriate.
    • Red text is used for votecounts, warnings, and other announcements by the moderators. It is also used for mafia flavor-related text.
    • Blue text is used for activity checks, confirm counts, and other non-essential posts by the moderators.
    • Green text is used for town flavor-related text.
    • Purple text is used for third-party flavor-related text.
    • This orange is ostriches88’s moderator color, and this green is duke_of_wedgewood’s moderator color.
    • Custom shades of these colors may be used for aesthetic purposes.
    • Current custom shades are: Town flavor green v.2 (#2be368) and Activity Check blue (#005dff). For a full list of custom colors, click here.
    • The moderators may change the rules at any time. Major rule changes will be announced at the bottom of the OP. All changes will also be noted in the modlog and OCRSTM Version History.
    • Players will not be retroactively penalized for violating new rules.
    • Major changes to the OP may be made in between games. The most important changes will be noted in the OCRSTM Version History.
    • The moderators pledge to treat players fairly and keep moderator interventions to a minimum; however, we reserve the right to make the final judgement in the moderation of this game.

    MG303 OP is based on Overcomplicated RulesetTM (OCRSTM) 10.
    Current version: 10.0.1
    Version History

    OCRSTM 10 is available for public use. If you are interested in using OCRSTM in your own games, click here.
Setup
This game is based on the Matrix6 setup. This is a semi-open setup.

In this setup, there are 9 players. Before the game starts, the setup will be randomly chosen from 6 possible role combinations:
  • Setup 1: 1 Town Jailkeeper, 6 Vanilla Townies, 2 Mafia Goons
  • Setup 2: 1 Town Cop, 1 Town Doctor, 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Mafia Roleblocker, 1 Mafia Goon
  • Setup 3: 1 Town Tracker, 1 1-shot Bulletproof Townie, 5 Vanilla Townies, 2 Mafia Goons
  • Setup 4/A: 1 Town Jailkeeper, 1 1-shot Bulletproof Townie, 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Mafia Roleblocker, 1 Mafia Goon
  • Setup 5/B: 1 Town Cop, 6 Vanilla Townies, 2 Mafia Goons
  • Setup 6/C: 1 Town Doctor, 1 Town Tracker, 5 Vanilla Townies, 2 Mafia Goons
Other important setup mechanics:
  • This game uses Dawn start. Once all the players confirm by completing the confirm task, Dawn will end, Day 1 will start, and the EOD deadline will be 12 days from then.
  • Doctors cannot protect themselves two nights in a row.
  • A Mafia Roleblocker's action takes precedence over a Town Jailkeeper's action.
  • A Mafia Roleblocker can k!ll and roleblock on the same night.
  • Cop results are sane.
  • Trackers can differentiate between "No movement" and "No result."
  • Mafia have daychat.
  • Mafia factional k!lls (NKs) are not compulsive.
Role PMs
Vanilla Townie wrote:
Hello, [player]!

You are Charlotte (Vanilla Townie).

You are a member of a tightly-knit group of North Carolinians, and your goal is to rid yourselves of the South Carolinian imposters.

As the city of Charlotte is very bland you have no special powers. Your power lies in your observation skills, your voice, and your vote.

You win when all Mafia are k!lled, so your goal is to find them. Play towards your win condition, and good luck.

Link to thread
Town Cop wrote:
Hello, [player]!

You are Durham (Town Cop).

You are a member of a tightly-knit group of North Carolinians, and your goal is to rid yourselves of the South Carolinian imposters.

Since you were once a crime-infested and academic university town you have a special ability at determining who the perpetrators are. Once each night, you may PM the mods the name of a player. You will check their alignment, finding out whether they are Town or Mafia. You will be informed if your action was blocked.

You win when all Mafia are k!lled, so your goal is to find them. Play towards your win condition, and good luck.

Link to thread
Town Doctor wrote:
Hello, [player]!

You are Greensboro (Town Doctor).

You are a member of a tightly-knit group of North Carolinians, and your goal is to rid yourselves of the South Carolinian imposters.

As the city of Greensboro you have a special knack at keeping your friends safe. Once each night, you may PM the mods the name of a player. They will be protected from a night k!ll that night. You can save yourself, but you cannot do this two nights in a row.

You win when all Mafia are k!lled, so your goal is to find them. Play towards your win condition, and good luck.

Link to thread
1-Shot Bulletproof Townie wrote:
Hello, [player]!

You are Asheville (1-Shot Bulletproof Townie).

You are a member of a tightly-knit group of North Carolinians, and your goal is to rid yourselves of the South Carolinian imposters.

As Asheville you are very resilient to many things, such as hurricanes. You will survive a single night k!ll attempt by the mafia, but if you are shot again after that, you will be k!lled. You are not immune to the daily hang, your ability cannot be roleblocked, and you do not know if or when you are shot.

You win when all Mafia are k!lled, so your goal is to find them. Play towards your win condition, and good luck.

Link to thread
Town Tracker wrote:
Hello, [player]!

You are Winston-Salem (Town Tracker).

You are a member of a tightly-knit group of North Carolinians, and your goal is to rid yourselves of the South Carolinian imposters.

With your city's universities finance and business degrees you have a special ability at tracking what stocks, and others are doing. Once each night, you may PM the mods the name of a player. You will track them, finding out if they performed a night action and who their target was. You will be informed if your action was blocked.

You win when all Mafia are k!lled, so your goal is to find them. Play towards your win condition, and good luck.

Link to thread
Town Jailkeeper wrote:
Hello, [player]!

You are Raleigh (Town Jailkeeper).

You are a member of a tightly-knit group of North Carolinians, and your goal is to rid yourselves of the South Carolinian imposters.

You are the capital of the state and so have the power to both protect and stop subordinate cities. Once each night, you may PM the mods the name of a player. You will jailkeep them, protecting them from a night k!ll that night, but roleblocking any actions they may attempt. You cannot target yourself with this ability.

You win when all Mafia are k!lled, so your goal is to find them. Play towards your win condition, and good luck.

Link to thread
Mafia Goon wrote:
Hello, [player]!

You are Charleston (Mafia Goon).

You are a South Carolinian who has successfully infiltrated a group of North Carolinians. Your goal is to stay hidden, and take over the group.

As the biggest city in South Carolina you are ready to help defeat the North Carolinians. But with no special powers you must depend upon your partner and the factional k!ll you share with them.

Your partner is [player] ([role]), and you may discuss with them in this private forum while you are alive. The two of you will be able to choose a player to k!ll each night, as well as decide who makes the k!ll.

You win when the Mafia are the only ones left, or if nothing can prevent this, so your goal is to stay hidden and k!ll Town. Play towards your win condition, and good luck.

Link to thread
Mafia Roleblocker wrote:
Hello, [player]!

You are Columbia (Mafia Roleblocker).

You are a South Carolinian who has successfully infiltrated a group of North Carolinians. Your goal is to stay hidden, and take over the group.

As the capital of South Carolina you have the great power to stop less important cities from messing with you and others. Once each night, you may PM the mods the name of a player. You will roleblock them, and any actions they attempt that night will fail. Your action takes precedence over the Town Jailkeeper, if applicable.

Your partner is [player] ([role]), and you may discuss with them in this private forum while you are alive. The two of you will be able to choose a player to k!ll each night, as well as decide who makes the k!ll.

You win when the Mafia are the only ones left, or if nothing can prevent this, so your goal is to stay hidden and k!ll Town. Play towards your win condition, and good luck.

Link to thread

[*]For the purposes of voting, I (capital i) and l (lowercase L) are considered identical. 
[*]For example, both [b]Vote: mathjay[/b] and [b]Vote: mAtHjAy[/b] are valid votes, but [b]vote: mathjay[/b], [b]Vote: jay[/b], [b]Vote: mathjay.[/b], [b]Vote:mathjay[/b], Vote: [b]mathjay[/b], and [b][i]Vote: mathjay[/i][/b] are invalid. 
[*]Votes followed by extraneous punctuation that is similarly formatted, even with separate tags, are invalid. 
[*]Votes in hide, tip, or quote tags do not count.
[*]Vote to hang no one by posting [b]Vote: No Hang[/b].
[*]Unvote by posting [b]Unvote[/b]. You do not have to unvote before voting again. 
[*]Votecounts will be posted periodically. Players may request a new VC in bold or in their role PM. 
[*]Invalid votes will not count under any circumstances. Consider notifying the mods in your role PM if you want to purposely fakevote.
[*]Valid votes will always be counted unless they are clearly unintentional. There is no guarantee that an accidental vote will not be counted.
[*]Votes will be carried over if a player is replaced. 
[*]The Not Voting section should be ignored if it contradicts another part of the votecount (VC). 
[*]If an error in the votecount is not pointed out within 2 days or by the next EOD, whichever is sooner, the incorrect VC will become official.
[*]This game uses deadline hang: the player with the most votes at the EOD (End of Day) deadline will be hanged.
[*]If two or more players have the same number of votes at the EOD deadline, the player who reached that number of votes first will be hanged. Details are in the Moderation Policies tab. 
[*]If no votes have been cast by the deadline, no one will be hanged. 
[*]Starting Day 2, if a player is voted by the majority of living players, then that player will be hanged immediately; and if No Hang is voted by half of living players, a No Hang will occur.
[*]Upon reaching LYLO (Hang-or-Lose), votes will be locked, meaning that they cannot be changed.
[*]Deadline hang is off at LYLO. If a majority hang is not reached by the deadline, the day will end without a hang, resulting in an automatic win for the mafia team. 
[*]The moment the deadline occurs, no further votes will be counted. Details are in the Moderation Policies tab. 
[*]If the deadline has passed or a hammer has occurred and the EOD has not been declared, the game will proceed into twilight. Players may still post, but no more votes or unvotes will be counted.
[*]Once the moderators declare the EOD, the person hanged will be officially eliminated and their role revealed. Night will then immediately begin.[/list][/hide]
[*][hide=Activity]
[b]This tab covers deadlines, activity rules, V/LAs, and replacements.[/b]


[list][*]All times will be in Eastern Standard Time (EST, UTC-5) or Eastern Daylight Time (EDT, UTC-4) as applicable, unless otherwise noted.
[*]Each day phase will be 288 hours (12 days), and each night phase will be 48 hours (2 days). 
[*]The moderators may extend deadlines at their discretion.
[*]The current deadline can be found in the bottom of the OP (Original Post, or post #1). 
[*]If a player with an NA (Night Action) anticipates being unavailable during the night phase, they may submit any night actions to be performed in advance. 
[*]If a player with an NA has not submitted their action by 24 hours before the night deadline, a reminder will be sent. 
[*]If the moderators do not receive an NA submission from a player by the night deadline, the player will not perform an action that night.
[*]Night results will be sent after the night deadline.
[*]Night will end when the moderators make a post declaring the end of night (EON). You may not post in the thread until then, even if the night deadline has passed. Instead, PM the mods for updates if they miss a deadline. 
[*]Players who are temporarily unable to fulfill activity requirements can declare a V/LA (Vacation/Long Absence) by posting the V/LA dates in [b]bold[/b] in-thread or in their role PM. Players on a V/LA will not be prodded. 
[*]V/LAs should be shorter than one week. For longer absences, contact the moderators for options. 
[*]Players who no longer wish to or are unable to play in this game can request replacement by posting [b]Request Replacement[/b] in-thread or in their role PM. 
[*]Players who have replaced out generally will not be allowed to replace back in. 
[*]Players will receive their first PM prod if they have not made a game-advancing post for 48 hours and are not on V/LA. Details on "game-advancing post" are in the Moderation Policies tab.
[*]Players will receive their second PM prod if they have not made a game-advancing post for 72 hours and are not on V/LA. 
[*]Players will be replaced if they have not made a game-advancing post for 96 hours and are not on V/LA, unless they post before the moderators find a replacement. 
[*]If a player receives 4 PM Prods, they will be replaced.
[*]If a player reaches replacement status more than once, they will be replaced.
[*]Activity Checks (ACs) will be posted periodically. Players may request a new AC in bold or in their role PM. 
[*]Activity will reset overnight. Players will not receive prods between twilight and daystart. 
[*]If you have access to a private topic, you may request that a partner be poked. The moderators will send a notification via PM to the specified player, but this will not count as an official prod.
[*]If a player is postbanned by the AoPS Sheriff, they will be replaced. Details are in the Moderation Policies tab.[list][/hide]
[*][hide=Integrity and Etiquette]
[b]This tab covers integrity recommendations, player etiquette, warning limits, and examples of inconsiderate gameplay.[/b]


[list][*]Do not gamethrow. 
[*]Do not discuss player alignments based on V/LAs, replacements, warnings, force replacements, modk!lls, or other interactions with moderators. 
[*]Do not discuss rule violations, especially violations by other players in the thread. If you wish, contact the moderators privately through your role PM or Discord. 
[*]Any significant, game-related, and intentionally exclusive player-to-player communication outside of moderator-sanctioned channels is prohibited.
[*]Do not publicly reference OGI (Out of Game Information) in reads or create situations outside the thread that players could reasonably consider alignment indicative.[/list]

The above rules are attempts to preserve the spirit of the game and should not affect most players. For specific details and examples, [url=https://aops.com/community/p24617334]click here[/url]. 


[list][*]Do not make personal attacks. You may attack a player's opinion, but do not demean the player themselves. 
[*]Do not spam.
[*]Follow AoPS Community Guidelines and the AoPS Terms of Service.
[*]Be mature and use common sense.
[*]Behavior that disrupts the game for players and makes it unreasonably hard for them to read and play may result in a warning.
[b]Causing significant disruptions to gameplay may result in a force replacement at the moderators' discretion. 
[*]For minor rule infractions, first-timers can receive up to three warnings, while all other players can receive up to two. A player may receive multiple warnings for a single infraction.[/list]

The above rules broadly address most etiquette issues. For specific details and examples of improper behavior, [url=https://aops.com/community/p24617342]click here[/url].[/hide]

[*][b][url=https://aops.com/community/p24617357]- Moderation Policies -[/url][/b]

[*][hide=Moderator Information]
[b]This tab provides technical information about this game.[/b]


[list][*]ostriches88 and duke_of_wedgewood are the moderators of Mafia Game 303.
[*]Other interns have contributed to flavor, setup, OCRSTM, feedback, and management of the private chat discord system.
[*]This game uses the Unpaid Intern Central consolidated private chat system. Participants of this game will be granted access as appropriate.
[*][color=#FF0000]Red text[/color] is used for votecounts, warnings, and other announcements by the moderators. It is also used for mafia flavor-related text.
[*][color=#0000FF]Blue text[/color] is used for activity checks, confirm counts, and other non-essential posts by the moderators. 
[*][color=#00FF00]Green text[/color] is used for town flavor-related text.

[*][color=#f50]This orange[/color] is ostriches88’s moderator color, and [color=#6AA84F]this green[/color] is duke_of_wedgewood’s moderator color.
[*]Custom shades of these colors may be used for aesthetic purposes. 
[*]Current custom shades are: Town flavor green v.2 ([b][color=#2be368]#2be368[/color][/b]) and Activity Check blue ([b][color=#005dff]#005dff[/color][/b]). For a full list of custom colors, [url=https://aops.com/community/p24617373]click here[/url]. 
[*]The moderators may change the rules at any time. Major rule changes will be announced at the bottom of the OP. All changes will also be noted in the modlog and OCRSTM Version History. 
[*]Players will not be retroactively penalized for violating new rules. 
[*]Major changes to the OP may be made in between games. The most important changes will be noted in the OCRSTM Version History.

[hide=Setup]This game is based on the [url=http://bit.ly/m6msw]Matrix6[/url] setup. This is a semi-open setup.

In this setup, there are 9 players. Before the game starts, the setup will be randomly chosen from 6 possible role combinations:
[list][*][b]Setup 1:[/b] 1 Town Jailkeeper, 6 Vanilla Townies, 2 Mafia Goons
[*][b]Setup 2:[/b] 1 Town Cop, 1 Town Doctor, 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Mafia Roleblocker, 1 Mafia Goon
[*][b]Setup 3:[/b] 1 Town Tracker, 1 1-shot Bulletproof Townie, 5 Vanilla Townies, 2 Mafia Goons
[*][b]Setup 4/A:[/b] 1 Town Jailkeeper, 1 1-shot Bulletproof Townie, 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Mafia Roleblocker, 1 Mafia Goon
[*][b]Setup 5/B:[/b] 1 Town Cop, 6 Vanilla Townies, 2 Mafia Goons
[*][b]Setup 6/C:[/b] 1 Town Doctor, 1 Town Tracker, 5 Vanilla Townies, 2 Mafia Goons[/list]
Other important setup mechanics: 
[list][*]This game uses Dawn start. Once all the players confirm by completing the confirm task, Dawn will end, Day 1 will start, and the EOD deadline will be 12 days from then. 
[*]Doctors cannot protect themselves two nights in a row.
[*]A Mafia Roleblocker's action takes precedence over a Town Jailkeeper's action.
[*]A Mafia Roleblocker can k!ll and roleblock on the same night.
[*]Cop results are sane.
[*]Trackers can differentiate between "No movement" and "No result." 
[*]Mafia have daychat.
[*]Mafia factional k!lls (NKs) are not compulsive. [/list]

mxsail wrote:
What's RQS? Anyway hi everyone, excited to begin my first mafia game

hi!

Since nobody's shown up with a proper entrance post I'll try to explain the most important early-game concepts (disclaimer I have strong opinionsTM on RVS but i'll try to remain neutral)

RQS is basically when somebody gives a list of questions for everybody to answer (typically like introductory information but it can be used for trying to find mafia in theory)

RVS is the typical early-game process where people make random votes on players for arbitrary reasons in an effort to kickstart the solving process if you're looking for more than that ask somebody else

e.g. "how did mxsail not figure out that NC means north carolina in a game called North Carolina Mafia Vote: mxsail"

also before scan gets his entrance post in: solving is the main gameplay of mafia & it's trying to make reads on other players by thinking about if their posts are more likely to come from town or mafia (other SE's/IC's might disagree about the particulars of what solving/reads are but it's something like that)

also everybody should read the rules

post_rendered:
hi everyone, you can call me scan, pronouns idc

feel free to ask me or the other two players (NDMath, ad333) any questions you have, although you may also look through this list of common terminology
RVS: random voting stage, in which people just vote each other for fun
RQS: random questioning stage, in which people make up questions for others to answer for fun
twilight: refers to a limbo stage between day and night. during twilight, day is supposed to end (due to either a player being hanged or deadline being reached), but it has not because the moderator isn't online yet to announce the end of day. usually you can get away with posting during twilight

claim: revealing your role (the green or red colored text in the private message ostriches sent you)
fakeclaim: falsely identify as another role that is not yours for strategic purposes
counterclaim: revealing your role to be identical to a role another player has claimed (therefore arguing that said player is fakeclaiming)
eliminate/elimination: usually interchangeable with hang
krill: usually refers to mafia's factional nightkill, but can also refer to hanging someone

alignment: refers to a player's team, which can be either town or mafia in this game
village: interchangeable with town
scum/wolf: interchangeable with mafia
scumteam/wolfteam: the two mafia players who make up the mafia team
mafia chat/scumchat/wolfchat: refers to a private forum (or discord server) in which the two members of the mafia team can communicate freely and secretly
townslip: when a player accidentally reveals themselves as town, due to ignorance of public information they should have known if they were mafia (for example, not knowing the number of mafia-aligned players in a game)
scumslip/wolfslip: when a player accidentally reveals them as mafia due to a mistake (not to be confused with tmi)
towny/scummy: when a player has done something that makes them seem like town/mafia (in particular, scummy can be seen as a synonym to suspicious)
conftown/confscum: short for "confirmed town/mafia"
townread/scumread: a player who the user of the term confidently believes to be aligned with town/mafia (can be also used as a verb)
townlean/scumlean: a player who the user of the term believes to be aligned with town/mafia, but is not certain in so (can be also used as a verb)

tmi: too much information, commonly used as an argument for a player being mafia because they know too much
sheeping: the act of agreeing with another's conclusions without stated reasoning. commonly associated with being mafia
opportunistic: (in simple terms) an argument for a player being mafia because they are playing aggressively
reaction test/RT: the act of asking another player a question, hoping to gain information about that player out of their reaction

lylo: [hang] or lose, meaning that town will lose unless they correctly eliminate a mafia today. this can happen with 3 town and 2 mafia alive, or with 2 town and 1 mafia alive
mylo: mis[hang] or lose, meaning that town will lose if they incorrectly eliminate a town today. the difference between this and lylo is that 1. town can choose to no hang and survive 2. there is 1 more town alive in mylo situations than in lylo situations (i.e. 4 town and 2 mafia alive)
eylo/cylo: interchangeable with lylo
f3: final three, meaning a scenario where only 3 players are alive (the number can be replaced with something other than 3; for example, f5 would refer to a scenario where only 5 players are alive)

wagon: a collection of votes on the same player
L-1/E-1: refers to the situation in which a player is one vote away from being hanged. (you can also replace the number with something other than 1; for example, l-2 means that a player is two votes around being eliminated). this is only relevant when majority hang is on (see below)
majority hang/elimination: refers to the rule that if one player receives a strict majority of all votes in play, they will be voted out and day will end immediately. if this rule is not enabled, a player will not be voted out even if every other player votes them
deadline hang/elimination: refers to the rule that if deadline. if this rule is not enabled, no player will be eliminated when deadline is reached
locked votes: refers to the rule that players cannot change their vote once they have placed one vote on someone. this rule is not enabled until lylo
hammer: refers to the final vote on a player that caused them to be hanged. can be also used as a verb
selfvote: refers to the action of one player voting themselves
sleep: interchangeable with no hang/elimination
policy hang/PL: when a player is hanged for being too annoying or otherwise being detrimental to town, even if they are not seen as suspicious

nd: refers to the player NDMath
ad: refers to the player ad333
i compiled

right now there's not much i want yall to do, aside from
1. try to be active once at least every 1-2 days (it's fine if you can't)
2. don't reveal any information about your role (if someone asks you anything about your role this early, you may assume that they are joking)
post_canonical:
hi everyone, you can call me scan, pronouns idc

feel free to ask me or the other two players (NDMath, ad333) any questions you have, although you may also look through this list of [hide=common terminology]
RVS: random voting stage, in which people just vote each other for fun
RQS: random questioning stage, in which people make up questions for others to answer for fun
twilight: refers to a limbo stage between day and night. during twilight, day is supposed to end (due to either a player being hanged or deadline being reached), but it has not because the moderator isn't online yet to announce the end of day. usually you can get away with posting during twilight

claim: revealing your role (the green or red colored text in the private message ostriches sent you)
fakeclaim: falsely identify as another role that is not yours for strategic purposes
counterclaim: revealing your role to be identical to a role another player has claimed (therefore arguing that said player is fakeclaiming)
eliminate/elimination: usually interchangeable with hang
krill: usually refers to mafia's factional nightkill, but can also refer to hanging someone

alignment: refers to a player's team, which can be either town or mafia in this game
village: interchangeable with town
scum/wolf: interchangeable with mafia
scumteam/wolfteam: the two mafia players who make up the mafia team
mafia chat/scumchat/wolfchat: refers to a private forum (or discord server) in which the two members of the mafia team can communicate freely and secretly
townslip: when a player accidentally reveals themselves as town, due to ignorance of public information they should have known if they were mafia (for example, not knowing the number of mafia-aligned players in a game)
scumslip/wolfslip: when a player accidentally reveals them as mafia due to a mistake (not to be confused with tmi)
towny/scummy: when a player has done something that makes them seem like town/mafia (in particular, scummy can be seen as a synonym to suspicious)
conftown/confscum: short for "confirmed town/mafia"
townread/scumread: a player who the user of the term confidently believes to be aligned with town/mafia (can be also used as a verb)
townlean/scumlean: a player who the user of the term believes to be aligned with town/mafia, but is not certain in so (can be also used as a verb)

tmi: too much information, commonly used as an argument for a player being mafia because they know too much
sheeping: the act of agreeing with another's conclusions without stated reasoning. commonly associated with being mafia
opportunistic: (in simple terms) an argument for a player being mafia because they are playing aggressively
reaction test/RT: the act of asking another player a question, hoping to gain information about that player out of their reaction

lylo: [hang] or lose, meaning that town will lose unless they correctly eliminate a mafia today. this can happen with 3 town and 2 mafia alive, or with 2 town and 1 mafia alive
mylo: mis[hang] or lose, meaning that town will lose if they incorrectly eliminate a town today. the difference between this and lylo is that 1. town can choose to no hang and survive 2. there is 1 more town alive in mylo situations than in lylo situations (i.e. 4 town and 2 mafia alive)
eylo/cylo: interchangeable with lylo
f3: final three, meaning a scenario where only 3 players are alive (the number can be replaced with something other than 3; for example, f5 would refer to a scenario where only 5 players are alive)

wagon: a collection of votes on the same player
L-1/E-1: refers to the situation in which a player is one vote away from being hanged. (you can also replace the number with something other than 1; for example, l-2 means that a player is two votes around being eliminated). this is only relevant when majority hang is on (see below)
majority hang/elimination: refers to the rule that if one player receives a strict majority of all votes in play, they will be voted out and day will end immediately. if this rule is not enabled, a player will not be voted out even if every other player votes them
deadline hang/elimination: refers to the rule that if deadline. if this rule is not enabled, no player will be eliminated when deadline is reached
locked votes: refers to the rule that players cannot change their vote once they have placed one vote on someone. this rule is not enabled until lylo
hammer: refers to the final vote on a player that caused them to be hanged. can be also used as a verb
selfvote: refers to the action of one player voting themselves
sleep: interchangeable with no hang/elimination 
policy hang/PL: when a player is hanged for being too annoying or otherwise being detrimental to town, even if they are not seen as suspicious

nd: refers to the player NDMath
ad: refers to the player ad333
[/hide] i compiled

right now there's not much i want yall to do, aside from
1. try to be active once at least every 1-2 days (it's fine if you can't)
2. don't reveal any information about your role (if someone asks you anything about your role this early, you may assume that they are joking)

C) we have agreed to massclaim (in newbie games, this typically happens day 3.

I feel like at least in output your game-related posts are largely either
1. overly aware
2. overly restrained

& particularly the shift between the two is kind of odd; I can argaubly chalk more of the restrained stuff (particularly the following post) to "newbie game voice" but that still makes the contrast odd
scannose wrote:
hi everyone, you can call me scan, pronouns idc

feel free to ask me or the other two players (NDMath, ad333) any questions you have, although you may also look through this list of common terminology
RVS: random voting stage, in which people just vote each other for fun
RQS: random questioning stage, in which people make up questions for others to answer for fun
twilight: refers to a limbo stage between day and night. during twilight, day is supposed to end (due to either a player being hanged or deadline being reached), but it has not because the moderator isn't online yet to announce the end of day. usually you can get away with posting during twilight

claim: revealing your role (the green or red colored text in the private message ostriches sent you)
fakeclaim: falsely identify as another role that is not yours for strategic purposes
counterclaim: revealing your role to be identical to a role another player has claimed (therefore arguing that said player is fakeclaiming)
eliminate/elimination: usually interchangeable with hang
krill: usually refers to mafia's factional nightkill, but can also refer to hanging someone

alignment: refers to a player's team, which can be either town or mafia in this game
village: interchangeable with town
scum/wolf: interchangeable with mafia
scumteam/wolfteam: the two mafia players who make up the mafia team
mafia chat/scumchat/wolfchat: refers to a private forum (or discord server) in which the two members of the mafia team can communicate freely and secretly
townslip: when a player accidentally reveals themselves as town, due to ignorance of public information they should have known if they were mafia (for example, not knowing the number of mafia-aligned players in a game)
scumslip/wolfslip: when a player accidentally reveals them as mafia due to a mistake (not to be confused with tmi)
towny/scummy: when a player has done something that makes them seem like town/mafia (in particular, scummy can be seen as a synonym to suspicious)
conftown/confscum: short for "confirmed town/mafia"
townread/scumread: a player who the user of the term confidently believes to be aligned with town/mafia (can be also used as a verb)
townlean/scumlean: a player who the user of the term believes to be aligned with town/mafia, but is not certain in so (can be also used as a verb)

tmi: too much information, commonly used as an argument for a player being mafia because they know too much
sheeping: the act of agreeing with another's conclusions without stated reasoning. commonly associated with being mafia
opportunistic: (in simple terms) an argument for a player being mafia because they are playing aggressively
reaction test/RT: the act of asking another player a question, hoping to gain information about that player out of their reaction

lylo: [hang] or lose, meaning that town will lose unless they correctly eliminate a mafia today. this can happen with 3 town and 2 mafia alive, or with 2 town and 1 mafia alive
mylo: mis[hang] or lose, meaning that town will lose if they incorrectly eliminate a town today. the difference between this and lylo is that 1. town can choose to no hang and survive 2. there is 1 more town alive in mylo situations than in lylo situations (i.e. 4 town and 2 mafia alive)
eylo/cylo: interchangeable with lylo
f3: final three, meaning a scenario where only 3 players are alive (the number can be replaced with something other than 3; for example, f5 would refer to a scenario where only 5 players are alive)

wagon: a collection of votes on the same player
L-1/E-1: refers to the situation in which a player is one vote away from being hanged. (you can also replace the number with something other than 1; for example, l-2 means that a player is two votes around being eliminated). this is only relevant when majority hang is on (see below)
majority hang/elimination: refers to the rule that if one player receives a strict majority of all votes in play, they will be voted out and day will end immediately. if this rule is not enabled, a player will not be voted out even if every other player votes them
deadline hang/elimination: refers to the rule that if deadline. if this rule is not enabled, no player will be eliminated when deadline is reached
locked votes: refers to the rule that players cannot change their vote once they have placed one vote on someone. this rule is not enabled until lylo
hammer: refers to the final vote on a player that caused them to be hanged. can be also used as a verb
selfvote: refers to the action of one player voting themselves
sleep: interchangeable with no hang/elimination
policy hang/PL: when a player is hanged for being too annoying or otherwise being detrimental to town, even if they are not seen as suspicious

nd: refers to the player NDMath
ad: refers to the player ad333
i compiled

right now there's not much i want yall to do, aside from
1. try to be active once at least every 1-2 days (it's fine if you can't)
2. don't reveal any information about your role (if someone asks you anything about your role this early, you may assume that they are joking)

Hmm, I’m not sure your approach is all that helpful, CBC. If you throw out a scumread without explaining it, it doesn’t really give others much to work with or respond to. Like, if someone just said “you’re mafia” out of the blue, my first instinct would be to ask why, not to defend myself- because there’s nothing solid to push back on. It feels like it shuts down discussion more than it encourages it.

That said, I do think trying to get reactions is a good idea in general - it just works better when there’s at least a little reasoning behind it. I’m not getting super strong scum vibes from you yet, but the explanation felt kind of like a backfill more than something natural.

I also agree with NDMath on the ad-scan thing. Something about it felt weirdly flat - like they were going through the motions. If it were town vs town, I’d expect more intensity or frustration. Instead, it just felt… kind of performative? It makes me wonder if it could actually be w/w.

That said, I’m not fully sold on everything in Scannose’s response either (#205). He points out that ad only used one quote to support a key part of his scumread, but most of Scan’s rebuttal also leans pretty heavily on one quote and tone. Also, the argument that active players shouldn’t be pressured feels a bit odd - sure, activity is helpful, but catching scum should still come first, even if they’re driving discussion. If anything, activity should invite more scrutiny, not less.

And with the bit about SEs focusing more on newbies—I don’t know, isn’t pushing and being pushed part of what helps everyone learn? I don’t think ad was out of line for questioning you if he thought something felt off. Plus, you mention the idea that a newbie could be scum but say you’re ignoring it unless someone else brings a case. That kind of feels like taking a back seat when town really needs more people pushing things forward. Maybe I’m overthinking it, but some of this just didn’t sit quite right with me.

post_rendered:
NDMath wrote:
Power Roles
Cop - If you get a guilty you'll claim it tomorrow and likely get attacked the next night regardless of who it is, so to determine your target you must look at who is the most beneficial to get an innocent result on. Checking the person the mafia attacked is a waste of a check. Investigating the person we eliminate tomorrow also isn't very beneficial as you'll have to claim sooner than ideal to save them.

Tracker - If the elimination flips town, there are 2 mafia alive meaning your only useful result is tracking the person who commits the attack. If the elimination flips mafia, read the part about the cop.

Doctor - You either wanna protect the other power role as a guard against the worst scenario, or the player most likely to be targeted to block the mafia attack.

Jailkeeper - You are the major power role in this setup if randed and that + the nature of the jk makes there not really a wrong way to use your action. Main goal is to block the mafia attack in some way.

1*BP - Sit tight.
^this is my usual pr advice for newbie games edited for terminology

If scum k!lled ND to silence a "suspicion" like that, it’s a stretch - especially since ND had much firmer townreads and more vocal slots on his list.

So, saying “ND’s death reflects badly on me” feels like reverse-engineering a read to match the NK, when his actual words don’t support a strong suspicion.

scannose wrote:
hi everyone, you can call me scan, pronouns idc

feel free to ask me or the other two players (NDMath, ad333) any questions you have, although you may also look through this list of common terminology
RVS: random voting stage, in which people just vote each other for fun
RQS: random questioning stage, in which people make up questions for others to answer for fun
twilight: refers to a limbo stage between day and night. during twilight, day is supposed to end (due to either a player being hanged or deadline being reached), but it has not because the moderator isn't online yet to announce the end of day. usually you can get away with posting during twilight

claim: revealing your role (the green or red colored text in the private message ostriches sent you)
fakeclaim: falsely identify as another role that is not yours for strategic purposes
counterclaim: revealing your role to be identical to a role another player has claimed (therefore arguing that said player is fakeclaiming)
eliminate/elimination: usually interchangeable with hang
krill: usually refers to mafia's factional nightkill, but can also refer to hanging someone

alignment: refers to a player's team, which can be either town or mafia in this game
village: interchangeable with town
scum/wolf: interchangeable with mafia
scumteam/wolfteam: the two mafia players who make up the mafia team
mafia chat/scumchat/wolfchat: refers to a private forum (or discord server) in which the two members of the mafia team can communicate freely and secretly
townslip: when a player accidentally reveals themselves as town, due to ignorance of public information they should have known if they were mafia (for example, not knowing the number of mafia-aligned players in a game)
scumslip/wolfslip: when a player accidentally reveals them as mafia due to a mistake (not to be confused with tmi)
towny/scummy: when a player has done something that makes them seem like town/mafia (in particular, scummy can be seen as a synonym to suspicious)
conftown/confscum: short for "confirmed town/mafia"
townread/scumread: a player who the user of the term confidently believes to be aligned with town/mafia (can be also used as a verb)
townlean/scumlean: a player who the user of the term believes to be aligned with town/mafia, but is not certain in so (can be also used as a verb)

tmi: too much information, commonly used as an argument for a player being mafia because they know too much
sheeping: the act of agreeing with another's conclusions without stated reasoning. commonly associated with being mafia
opportunistic: (in simple terms) an argument for a player being mafia because they are playing aggressively
reaction test/RT: the act of asking another player a question, hoping to gain information about that player out of their reaction

lylo: [hang] or lose, meaning that town will lose unless they correctly eliminate a mafia today. this can happen with 3 town and 2 mafia alive, or with 2 town and 1 mafia alive
mylo: mis[hang] or lose, meaning that town will lose if they incorrectly eliminate a town today. the difference between this and lylo is that 1. town can choose to no hang and survive 2. there is 1 more town alive in mylo situations than in lylo situations (i.e. 4 town and 2 mafia alive)
eylo/cylo: interchangeable with lylo
f3: final three, meaning a scenario where only 3 players are alive (the number can be replaced with something other than 3; for example, f5 would refer to a scenario where only 5 players are alive)

wagon: a collection of votes on the same player
L-1/E-1: refers to the situation in which a player is one vote away from being hanged. (you can also replace the number with something other than 1; for example, l-2 means that a player is two votes around being eliminated). this is only relevant when majority hang is on (see below)
majority hang/elimination: refers to the rule that if one player receives a strict majority of all votes in play, they will be voted out and day will end immediately. if this rule is not enabled, a player will not be voted out even if every other player votes them
deadline hang/elimination: refers to the rule that if deadline. if this rule is not enabled, no player will be eliminated when deadline is reached
locked votes: refers to the rule that players cannot change their vote once they have placed one vote on someone. this rule is not enabled until lylo
hammer: refers to the final vote on a player that caused them to be hanged. can be also used as a verb
selfvote: refers to the action of one player voting themselves
sleep: interchangeable with no hang/elimination
policy hang/PL: when a player is hanged for being too annoying or otherwise being detrimental to town, even if they are not seen as suspicious

nd: refers to the player NDMath
ad: refers to the player ad333
i compiled

right now there's not much i want yall to do, aside from
1. try to be active once at least every 1-2 days (it's fine if you can't)
2. don't reveal any information about your role (if someone asks you anything about your role this early, you may assume that they are joking)

NDMath++ (confirmed town) directly supported Scan early on, which lends towncred to Scan’s initial tone and approach.

Despite growing frustration with the low activity from others, Scan has kept trying to move the game forward. Their tone suggests genuine disappointment at the current threadstate — which scum would be more likely to exploit, not complain about. That level of transparency feels genuine and towny.

If I had one critique, it's that Scan could push harder and follow through more aggressively on their suspects. There's a balance between mentoring and solving, and I’d like to see Scan lean more into the latter.

Final D2 Read:
5% Scum / 95% Town — Strong townread based on tone, transparency, and genuine frustration.

ad333++

Ad’s activity has been below expectations for an IC. It’s understandable to give newer players more space, but extended low activity has unfortunately contributed to the stagnant threadstate, which hasn’t helped newbie engagement or progression.

There isn’t much more to say. Ad’s activity levels are simply too low to formulate proper reads, but such lowposting is highly scum-indicative at an IC level of gameplay.

Final D2 Read:
33% Scum / 66% Town — Slight scumlean due to inactivity, but open to reevaluation if they contribute more.

Unicode_Master03B8

Unicode is a bit of a paradox. They’ve shown signs of having done their homework — referencing theory, asking smart questions — but haven’t applied that knowledge to form or act on their own reads.

This mismatch raises an eyebrow. Someone who’s researched mafia theory and terminology should be more willing to share takes or commit to pushes. Hesitance could come from nerves — especially as a newbie — but scum also benefit from cautious, fence-sitting behavior.

Unicode isn’t coasting per se, but they’re not pulling their weight either. I want to believe in them, but I'd like to see stronger stances and follow-through.

Final D2 Read:
25% Scum / 75% Town — light scumlean, but needs to step up or risk sliding into PoE.

Eric.s.lu

Eric has claimed to be busy with schoolwork — fair enough. While low activity is a factor, I don’t think he’s sheeping, as some have suggested.

following another player and not thinking on your own

His actions don’t align with classic bandwagoning behavior .

There was some early PR speculation from Eric, which might look odd, but I think that’s more of a newbie hiccup than a scumtell. I disagree with Scan’s push here — it feels slightly exaggerated and might just stem from frustration.

Still, Eric hasn’t shown initiative or given us much to work with. He’s not actively scummy, but he’s not towny either. Needs to contribute more.

Final D2 Read:
15% Scum / 85% Town — Very mild scumlean due to low output, but not a major concern yet.

aidan0626

Aidan has been a quieter presence compared to someone like Scannose, but his contributions still feel thoughtful and genuinely aimed at solving the game. He doesn’t post as frequently, but when he does, his posts tend to include clear reasoning and careful consideration of the threadstate. That’s the kind of playstyle that’s easy to overlook, but it adds a steady presence that benefits town.

He’s asked relevant questions and shown a willingness to reevaluate positions instead of tunneling or parroting others. That kind of flexibility is important, especially in a game where thread momentum has stalled.

While I’d like to see a bit more activity from Aidan overall, I don’t see anything to suggest a scum agenda. His tone feels natural, not performative, and he isn’t afraid to push back or disagree when needed. It’s clear he’s reading, thinking, and trying to sort through the noise — just doing so in a more reserved way than Scan.

Final D2 Read:
7% Scum / 93% Town — Solid townlean. Less active than Scan, but still clearly engaged and solving.

also, you pointed out Aidan is the most experience newbie. Well, then, if they were mafia, they would most likely know to follow an experienced player because from his experience he know people with most experience are usually the best player. that only strengthens the point of him following ad's

What your saying is still illogical:

1. The idea of providing cover by getting yourself hung doesn’t make sense from a scum POV
If we hang scum today, and the NK isn’t blocked or interfered with by a PR, we’re down to 1 mafia vs 4 town — potentially with PRs in play. The last mafia would then need to survive two consecutive day phases. Even with lucky mislynches, the odds of scum winning from there are low — around 8/15 assuming completely random votes. So “getting hung to cover a partner” is a losing strategy that experienced players wouldn't intentionally take.

2. We don’t need to pair-read them to act.
You’re saying Ad and Aidan might be partnered because they don’t look partnered — but that logic cuts both ways. It’s easy to construct “X is scum because of distancing” narratives in hindsight, but unless there's clear intent or coordination, it’s not solid. Scum rarely make themselves look scummy on purpose; even when bussing, they usually try to minimize their own exposure. No one wants to get hung as scum — it sucks, and it weakens the team.

If you think Ad is suspicious on her own, that’s astronger argument. But trying to frame it as deliberate scum theater or "bus-and-bait" feels like a stretch right now.

Thus, all this feels like an attempt to throw town in the wrong direction, providing cover and taking attention away from the real scumteam (i'm thinking Johann/Eric now). Now that it's clear my Johann hang won't go through, despite my best efforts (Trust me he is 100% scum) I will Vote: Eric.s.lu, as the second most likely scum.

post_rendered:
a few extra setup notes if ad flips town today

Click to reveal hidden text
cop: if you have a greencheck (you learned that some player is innocent) on night 1, one thing you may consider is to investigate another townie who you believe has a low chance of being krilled tonight. this may be helpful because if you survive to day 3 and both players you checked are alive, you can deduce that the two players you didn't check are mafia.
even if the player you checked n1 dies tonight, having a greencheck still provides information (although receiving a redcheck (learning that some player is guilty) is probably slightly better)

jailkeeper: you can still use your power on any player you think is reasonable, just be aware that 1sbp claims in jailkeeper games are not confirmed town unless some piece of information makes you certain that you are roleblocked (for example, if the player you jailed n1 died. this also holds with doctor claims in cop games, although i think that scenario is just less likely to happen)

I was strongly townreading Aidan on Day 2, but on Day 3, they've been quite impulsive. This behavior is unusual because Aidan previously maintained a calm attitude towards the game. Given that people can (and will) carefully orchestrate their thoughts in a text-based forum, stirring and invoking any tone they choose, Aidan may be trying to use Appeal-to-Emotion (ATE) to sway unsuspecting players who perceive Aidan's emotions as genuine.

Making an early vote at a critical time when votes are locked is never a good idea. Unless you are certain—something that scum players with their insider knowledge often are—it is illogical and incredibly risky to vote so early in a LyLo situation. While there may be justification for Aidan’s actions due to their potential role (assuming they aren't fakeclaiming), most players would still take the time to explain their reasoning to others and consult their faction before making such critical decisions.

Furthermore, the speed, decisiveness, and quick progression of Aidan’s actions imply that this may have been planned during the night phase. This has seriously diminished my trust in Aidan; however, I believe that the towncred he has built over the past three weeks cannot be undermined by a single incident, unless it’s an obvious mafiaslip.

In conclusion, I generally agree with Blight, but have a few additional points that are worth looking in to.

scannose wrote:
hi everyone, you can call me scan, pronouns idc

feel free to ask me or the other two players (NDMath, ad333) any questions you have, although you may also look through this list of common terminology
RVS: random voting stage, in which people just vote each other for fun
RQS: random questioning stage, in which people make up questions for others to answer for fun
twilight: refers to a limbo stage between day and night. during twilight, day is supposed to end (due to either a player being hanged or deadline being reached), but it has not because the moderator isn't online yet to announce the end of day. usually you can get away with posting during twilight

claim: revealing your role (the green or red colored text in the private message ostriches sent you)
fakeclaim: falsely identify as another role that is not yours for strategic purposes
counterclaim: revealing your role to be identical to a role another player has claimed (therefore arguing that said player is fakeclaiming)
eliminate/elimination: usually interchangeable with hang
krill: usually refers to mafia's factional nightkiII, but can also refer to hanging someone

alignment: refers to a player's team, which can be either town or mafia in this game
village: interchangeable with town
scum/wolf: interchangeable with mafia
scumteam/wolfteam: the two mafia players who make up the mafia team
mafia chat/scumchat/wolfchat: refers to a private forum (or discord server) in which the two members of the mafia team can communicate freely and secretly
townslip: when a player accidentally reveals themselves as town, due to ignorance of public information they should have known if they were mafia (for example, not knowing the number of mafia-aligned players in a game)
scumslip/wolfslip: when a player accidentally reveals them as mafia due to a mistake (not to be confused with tmi)
towny/scummy: when a player has done something that makes them seem like town/mafia (in particular, scummy can be seen as a synonym to suspicious)
conftown/confscum: short for "confirmed town/mafia"
townread/scumread: a player who the user of the term confidently believes to be aligned with town/mafia (can be also used as a verb)
townlean/scumlean: a player who the user of the term believes to be aligned with town/mafia, but is not certain in so (can be also used as a verb)

tmi: too much information, commonly used as an argument for a player being mafia because they know too much
sheeping: the act of agreeing with another's conclusions without stated reasoning. commonly associated with being mafia
opportunistic: (in simple terms) an argument for a player being mafia because they are playing aggressively
reaction test/RT: the act of asking another player a question, hoping to gain information about that player out of their reaction

lylo: [hang] or lose, meaning that town will lose unless they correctly eliminate a mafia today. this can happen with 3 town and 2 mafia alive, or with 2 town and 1 mafia alive
mylo: mis[hang] or lose, meaning that town will lose if they incorrectly eliminate a town today. the difference between this and lylo is that 1. town can choose to no hang and survive 2. there is 1 more town alive in mylo situations than in lylo situations (i.e. 4 town and 2 mafia alive)
eylo/cylo: interchangeable with lylo
f3: final three, meaning a scenario where only 3 players are alive (the number can be replaced with something other than 3; for example, f5 would refer to a scenario where only 5 players are alive)

wagon: a collection of votes on the same player
L-1/E-1: refers to the situation in which a player is one vote away from being hanged. (you can also replace the number with something other than 1; for example, l-2 means that a player is two votes around being eliminated). this is only relevant when majority hang is on (see below)
majority hang/elimination: refers to the rule that if one player receives a strict majority of all votes in play, they will be voted out and day will end immediately. if this rule is not enabled, a player will not be voted out even if every other player votes them
deadline hang/elimination: refers to the rule that if deadline. if this rule is not enabled, no player will be eliminated when deadline is reached
locked votes: refers to the rule that players cannot change their vote once they have placed one vote on someone. this rule is not enabled until lylo
hammer: refers to the final vote on a player that caused them to be hanged. can be also used as a verb
selfvote: refers to the action of one player voting themselves
sleep: interchangeable with no hang/elimination
policy hang/PL: when a player is hanged for being too annoying or otherwise being detrimental to town, even if they are not seen as suspicious

nd: refers to the player NDMath
ad: refers to the player ad333
i compiled

right now there's not much i want yall to do, aside from
1. try to be active once at least every 1-2 days (it's fine if you can't)
2. don't reveal any information about your role (if someone asks you anything about your role this early, you may assume that they are joking)

What your saying is still illogical:

1. The idea of providing cover by getting yourself hung doesn’t make sense from a scum POV
If we hang scum today, and the NK isn’t blocked or interfered with by a PR, we’re down to 1 mafia vs 4 town — potentially with PRs in play. The last mafia would then need to survive two consecutive day phases. Even with lucky mislynches, the odds of scum winning from there are low — around 8/15 assuming completely random votes. So “getting hung to cover a partner” is a losing strategy that experienced players wouldn't intentionally take.

2. We don’t need to pair-read them to act.

It’s easy to construct “X is scum because of distancing” narratives in hindsight, but unless there's clear intent or coordination, it’s not solid. That said, I do think trying to get reactions is a good idea in general - it just works better when there’s at least a little reasoning behind it. I’m not getting super strong scum vibes from you yet, but the explanation felt kind of like a backfill more than something natural.

I also agree with NDMath on the ad-scan thing. Something about it felt weirdly flat - like they were going through the motions. If it were town vs town, I’d expect more intensity or frustration. Instead, it just felt… kind of performative? It makes me wonder if it could actually be w/w.

He points out that ad only used one quote to support a key part of his scumread, but most of Scan’s rebuttal also leans pretty heavily on one quote and tone. If anything, activity should invite more scrutiny, not less.

And with the bit about SEs focusing more on newbies—I don’t know, isn’t pushing and being pushed part of what helps everyone learn? I don’t think ad was out of line for questioning you if he thought something felt off. Plus, you mention the idea that a newbie could be scum but say you’re ignoring it unless someone else brings a case. That kind of feels like taking a back seat when town really needs more people pushing things forward. Maybe I’m overthinking it, but some of this just didn’t sit quite right with me.

[b]Scannose+[/b]

Scan has remained active and engaged. Their early posts were well-written, constructive, and aimed at helping newer players understand mafia fundamentals.[quote=scannose]hi everyone, you can call me scan, pronouns idc

feel free to ask me or the other two players (NDMath, ad333) any questions you have, although you may also look through this list of [hide=common terminology]
RVS: random voting stage, in which people just vote each other for fun
RQS: random questioning stage, in which people make up questions for others to answer for fun
twilight: refers to a limbo stage between day and night. during twilight, day is supposed to end (due to either a player being hanged or deadline being reached), but it has not because the moderator isn't online yet to announce the end of day. usually you can get away with posting during twilight

claim: revealing your role (the green or red colored text in the private message ostriches sent you)
fakeclaim: falsely identify as another role that is not yours for strategic purposes
counterclaim: revealing your role to be identical to a role another player has claimed (therefore arguing that said player is fakeclaiming)
eliminate/elimination: usually interchangeable with hang
krill: usually refers to mafia's factional nightkiII, but can also refer to hanging someone

alignment: refers to a player's team, which can be either town or mafia in this game
village: interchangeable with town
scum/wolf: interchangeable with mafia
scumteam/wolfteam: the two mafia players who make up the mafia team
mafia chat/scumchat/wolfchat: refers to a private forum (or discord server) in which the two members of the mafia team can communicate freely and secretly
townslip: when a player accidentally reveals themselves as town, due to ignorance of public information they should have known if they were mafia (for example, not knowing the number of mafia-aligned players in a game)
scumslip/wolfslip: when a player accidentally reveals them as mafia due to a mistake (not to be confused with tmi)
towny/scummy: when a player has done something that makes them seem like town/mafia (in particular, scummy can be seen as a synonym to suspicious)
conftown/confscum: short for "confirmed town/mafia"
townread/scumread: a player who the user of the term confidently believes to be aligned with town/mafia (can be also used as a verb)
townlean/scumlean: a player who the user of the term believes to be aligned with town/mafia, but is not certain in so (can be also used as a verb)

tmi: too much information, commonly used as an argument for a player being mafia because they know too much
sheeping: the act of agreeing with another's conclusions without stated reasoning. commonly associated with being mafia
opportunistic: (in simple terms) an argument for a player being mafia because they are playing aggressively
reaction test/RT: the act of asking another player a question, hoping to gain information about that player out of their reaction

lylo: [hang] or lose, meaning that town will lose unless they correctly eliminate a mafia today. this can happen with 3 town and 2 mafia alive, or with 2 town and 1 mafia alive
mylo: mis[hang] or lose, meaning that town will lose if they incorrectly eliminate a town today. the difference between this and lylo is that 1. town can choose to no hang and survive 2. there is 1 more town alive in mylo situations than in lylo situations (i.e. 4 town and 2 mafia alive)
eylo/cylo: interchangeable with lylo
f3: final three, meaning a scenario where only 3 players are alive (the number can be replaced with something other than 3; for example, f5 would refer to a scenario where only 5 players are alive)

wagon: a collection of votes on the same player
L-1/E-1: refers to the situation in which a player is one vote away from being hanged. (you can also replace the number with something other than 1; for example, l-2 means that a player is two votes around being eliminated). this is only relevant when majority hang is on (see below)
majority hang/elimination: refers to the rule that if one player receives a strict majority of all votes in play, they will be voted out and day will end immediately. if this rule is not enabled, a player will not be voted out even if every other player votes them
deadline hang/elimination: refers to the rule that if deadline. if this rule is not enabled, no player will be eliminated when deadline is reached
locked votes: refers to the rule that players cannot change their vote once they have placed one vote on someone. this rule is not enabled until lylo
hammer: refers to the final vote on a player that caused them to be hanged. can be also used as a verb
selfvote: refers to the action of one player voting themselves
sleep: interchangeable with no hang/elimination 
policy hang/PL: when a player is hanged for being too annoying or otherwise being detrimental to town, even if they are not seen as suspicious

nd: refers to the player NDMath
ad: refers to the player ad333
[/hide] i compiled

right now there's not much i want yall to do, aside from
1. try to be active once at least every 1-2 days (it's fine if you can't)
2. don't reveal any information about your role (if someone asks you anything about your role this early, you may assume that they are joking)[/quote]

[b]What your saying is still illogical:[/b]

[b]1. The idea of providing cover by getting yourself hung doesn’t make sense from a scum POV[/b]
If we hang scum today, and the NK isn’t blocked or interfered with by a PR, we’re down to 1 mafia vs 4 town — potentially with PRs in play. The last mafia would then need to survive two consecutive day phases. Even with lucky mislynches, the odds of scum winning from there are low — around 8/15 assuming completely random votes. So “getting hung to cover a partner” is a losing strategy that experienced players wouldn't intentionally take.

[b]2. We don’t need to pair-read them to act.[/b]
You’re saying Ad and Aidan might be partnered because they don’t look partnered — but that logic cuts both ways. It’s easy to construct “X is scum because of distancing” narratives in hindsight, but unless there's clear intent or coordination, it’s not solid. Scum rarely make themselves look scummy on purpose; even when bussing, they usually try to minimize their own exposure. No one wants to get hung as scum — it sucks, and it weakens the team.

If you think Ad is suspicious on her own, that’s astronger argument. But trying to frame it as deliberate scum theater or "bus-and-bait" feels like a stretch right now.

Thus, all this feels like an attempt to throw town in the wrong direction, providing cover and taking attention away from the real scumteam (i'm thinking Johann/Eric now).
